structure.title: SECTION 1: Comprehensive Websites

nodes.title: Ready for Wildfire website (CAL FIRE)
nodes.summary:
This partial document, titled "RESOURCE GUIDE #3: Home Hardening and Defensible Space Resources for Residents," serves as a curated guide to user-friendly and up-to-date resources for homeowners on wildfire preparedness.

The document's introduction outlines its scope, which includes comprehensive websites, specific guides for home hardening and defensible space/landscaping, ember storm resources, videos, and diagrams.

The provided text focuses on "SECTION 1: Comprehensive Websites," detailing several recommended online resources:
*   **Ready for Wildfire (CAL FIRE):** Highlighted as the primary comprehensive website, it offers extensive public information on defensible space, home hardening, and evacuation. It provides resources for individual residents (including Spanish options, a "Low Cost Retrofit List," and self-assessment tools) and campaign toolkits for organizations.
*   **Preparing Your Home & Landscaping (U. California):** Offers detailed guidance for homeowners, supported by links to research literature.
*   **Fire Safe Marin:** Provides webpages on key topics like home hardening, fire-smart yards, and evacuation, often with embedded videos.
*   **Ready, Set, Go! (Woodside Fire Protection District):** Organizes materials into "ready" (home hardening and defensible space), "set," and "go" (evacuation), featuring short videos and one-page briefers.

nodes.title: Preparing Your Home & Landscaping (U. California)
nodes.summary:
This partial document is "RESOURCE GUIDE #3: Home Hardening and Defensible Space Resources for Residents." Its primary purpose is to provide homeowners with a curated list of user-friendly and up-to-date resources for wildfire preparedness, specifically focusing on home hardening and creating defensible space.

The guide outlines six sections of resources, though the provided text details only the first section: "Comprehensive Websites." This section highlights several key online resources, including:
*   **CAL FIRE's Ready for Wildfire website:** Recommended as a comprehensive resource covering defensible space, home hardening, and evacuation. It offers tools for both residents (e.g., "Low Cost Retrofit List," Wildfire Preparedness Plan, Defensible Space Self-Assessment) and organizations (multi-media campaign toolkits), with content available in English and Spanish.
*   **U. California's Preparing Your Home & Landscaping:** Provides guidance for homeowners, including priority actions and links to research literature.
*   **Fire Safe Marin website:** Offers information on home hardening, fire-smart yards, evacuation, and other preparedness tips, including embedded videos.
*   **Woodside Fire Protection District's Ready, Set, Go!:** Organizes materials into ready (home hardening and defensible space), set, and go (evacuation), featuring short videos and one-page briefers.

Overall, the document serves as a directory to essential online information and tools for residents and organizations to enhance wildfire safety and resilience.

nodes.title: Ready, Set, Go! (Woodside Fire Protection District)
nodes.summary:
This partial document is "RESOURCE GUIDE #3: Home Hardening and Defensible Space Resources for Residents," designed to provide user-friendly and up-to-date information on wildfire preparedness. It outlines six sections of resources, with the provided text covering the first two:

1.  **Comprehensive Websites:** This section lists and describes several recommended websites, such as CAL FIRE's Ready for Wildfire, U. California's Preparing Your Home & Landscaping, Fire Safe Marin, Woodside Fire Protection District's Ready, Set, Go!, and Santa Rosa's Property Owner Resource Library. These websites offer extensive guidance on defensible space, home hardening, evacuation, and preparedness, often including tools for both the public (e.g., self-assessments, low-cost retrofit lists) and organizations (e.g., campaign toolkits). Many resources are available in multiple languages, like Spanish.

2.  **Home Hardening – Document Guides:** This section details various document-based resources focused specifically on home hardening. Key guides include the "Wildfire Home Retrofit Guide" (a recommended 20-page guidebook with graphics and nuanced advice), the "Research Fact Sheet Series" (ten one-page fact sheets on specific topics like roofing, vents, and decks, often customizable), "Protect Your Property from Wildfire" (a detailed retrofit guide), and the "Wildfire Home Assessment & Checklist" (to identify vulnerabilities and outline mitigation actions).

nodes.title: Property Owner Resource Library (Santa Rosa)
nodes.summary:
This partial document, titled "RESOURCE GUIDE #3," serves as a compilation of resources for residents on "Home Hardening and Defensible Space" to prepare for wildfires. It lists various guides and online libraries, including:

*   **Property Owner Resource Library (Santa Rosa):** A city webpage offering guidance on wildfire preparedness, defensible space, home hardening, notification, evacuation, and property disclosures (AB 38).
*   **Home Hardening Document Guides:** A section detailing specific documents like:
    *   The "Wildfire Home Retrofit Guide," a recommended 20-page guidebook with clear guidance and graphics for public use.
    *   The "Research Fact Sheet Series," offering ten one-page fact sheets on key home hardening topics such as roofing, vents, decks, and fencing, with customizable versions.
    *   "Protect Your Property from Wildfire," a more detailed resource for in-depth information.
    *   The "Wildfire Home Assessment & Checklist," a guide to identify home vulnerabilities and outline mitigation actions with cost estimates.

Overall, the document provides a comprehensive list of practical resources to help homeowners understand, assess, and implement measures to protect their properties from wildfire.

structure.title: SECTION 2: Home Hardening – Document Guides

nodes.title: Wildfire Home Retrofit Guide
nodes.summary: This partial document, titled "RESOURCE GUIDE #3," provides a comprehensive list of resources for residents on home hardening and creating defensible space to prepare for wildfires. It includes links to city resources like the Santa Rosa Property Owner Resource Library, which covers topics such as WUI maps, notification, evacuation, and property disclosures. The document also details several "Home Hardening – Document Guides," including a recommended Wildfire Home Retrofit Guide, a Research Fact Sheet Series from IBHS and NFPA covering specific home components (e.g., roofing, vents, decks, fencing) with customizable versions, a more detailed "Protect Your Property from Wildfire" guide, and a "Wildfire Home Assessment & Checklist" to identify vulnerabilities and outline mitigation actions with cost estimates.

nodes.title: Protect Your Property from Wildfire (IBHS)

The document also details several "Home Hardening – Document Guides," including a recommended Wildfire Home Retrofit Guide, a Research Fact Sheet Series from IBHS and NFPA covering specific home components (e.g., roofing, vents, decks, fencing) with customizable versions, a more detailed "Protect Your Property from Wildfire" guide, and a "Wildfire Home Assessment & Checklist" to identify vulnerabilities and outline mitigation actions with cost estimates.

nodes.title: Wildfire Home Assessment & Checklist (IBHS)
nodes.summary:
This partial document, "RESOURCE GUIDE # 3: Home Hardening and Defensible Space Resources for Residents," serves as a comprehensive compilation of resources for property owners to prepare for and mitigate wildfire risks. It covers key areas such as:

*   **General Wildfire Preparedness:** Links to city resources, WUI maps, and guidance on notification, evacuation, and property disclosures.
*   **Home Hardening:** Provides a variety of document guides, retrofit guides, and fact sheets detailing specific actions and materials for making homes more resistant to wildfire, including topics like roofing, vents, decks, fencing, coatings, and skylights.
*   **Defensible Space & Landscaping:** Offers guides on creating and maintaining defensible space around properties, focusing on plant selection, landscaping approaches, the combustibility of mulches, and lists of fire-resistant plants.
*   **Ember Storm Resources:** Features guides and videos specifically addressing the threat of ember storms, outlining actions homeowners can take to protect their homes from ember ignition.
*   **Video Resources:** Recommends a YouTube channel with numerous videos on community education, fire-smart yards, home hardening, and general preparedness.

structure.title: SECTION 3: Defensible Space & Landscaping – Document Guides

nodes.title: Reducing the Vulnerability of Buildings to Wildfire: Vegetation and Landscaping Guidance (U. California)
nodes.summary:
This partial document is a resource guide (#3) focused on home hardening and creating defensible space to protect residences from wildfires. It is organized into three main sections:

1.  **Defensible Space & Landscaping:** This section provides document guides and websites offering advice on selecting and maintaining fire-resistant plants, understanding the combustibility of landscape mulches, and general landscaping approaches to reduce wildfire vulnerability. It includes resources from various universities and regional fire safety organizations.
2.  **Ember Storm Resources:** This section lists guides and a video specifically addressing how to harden homes against wildfire ember storms, identifying vulnerable areas, and actions homeowners can take to reduce risk.
3.  **Videos:** This section highlights a YouTube channel (Fire Safe Marin) as a comprehensive video resource covering community education, fire-smart yards, home hardening, and general wildfire preparedness.

2.  **Ember Storm Resources:** This section offers guides and a video on how to harden homes against wildfire ember storms, identifying areas prone to ember-driven fires, and actions homeowners can take to mitigate risk.
3.  **Videos:** This section highlights a YouTube account (Fire Safe Marin) that provides a comprehensive collection of videos on community education, fire-smart yards, home hardening, and general wildfire preparedness.

structure.title: SECTION 5: Videos

nodes.title: Fire Safe Marin – YouTube Account
nodes.summary:
This partial document, "RESOURCE GUIDE # 3: Home Hardening and Defensible Space Resources for Residents," provides a curated list of resources aimed at helping residents reduce wildfire risk. It covers:

*   **Defensible Space & Landscaping:** Guides on selecting and maintaining fire-resistant plants, understanding the combustibility of landscape mulches, and general landscaping approaches to reduce vulnerability to wildfire, including county-specific resources.
*   **Ember Storm Resources:** Information on hardening homes against ember storms, identifying areas prone to ember-driven wildfires, and a video demonstrating ember impact.
*   **Videos:** A YouTube channel offering a wide range of educational videos on community education, fire-smart yards, home hardening, and preparedness.
*   **Diagrams and Illustrations:** Key graphics illustrating concepts like home hardening in the Wildland-Urban Interface (WUI) and defensible space zones for both individual homes and neighborhoods.

structure.title: SECTION 6: Diagrams and Illustrations

nodes.title: WUI Concept for the Home
nodes.summary: This partial document, "RESOURCE GUIDE #3," focuses on "Home Hardening and Defensible Space Resources for Residents." Specifically, Section 6 provides a list of key diagrams and illustrations intended for public information and education. These graphics cover the "WUI Concept for the Home" (showing mitigation actions and best practices for hardening a home in the Wildland-Urban Interface), "Defensible Space Zones – Home" (illustrating zones around a single home), and "Defensible Space Zones - Neighborhood" (illustrating zones around multiple homes). The guide was compiled by the Association of Bay Area Governments in October 2021.

nodes.title: Defensible Space Zones – Home
nodes.summary: This partial document, "RESOURCE GUIDE #3," focuses on "Home Hardening and Defensible Space Resources for Residents." Specifically, it details "SECTION 6: Diagrams and Illustrations," which provides key visual aids for public information and education. The section lists three main types of graphics: one illustrating the Wildland-Urban Interface (WUI) concept for home hardening and best practices, another depicting defensible space zones around a single home, and a third showing defensible space zones for multiple homes within a neighborhood. The guide was compiled by the Association of Bay Area Governments in October 2021.
